summary: The staged slice is not safe to ship: the new reapply/logging phases can silently destroy queue/log state and still rely on unlocked read-modify-write cycles across the cron and own-port server paths.

findings.title: Reapply sweep can erase the entire review queue after any malformed or transiently unreadable `review-queue.json`
findings.recommendation: Do not treat queue-load failure as `[]`. Plumb a distinguished `{ok,error}` result out of `loadQueue`, skip persistence on unreadable/malformed input, and record an explicit queue-level failure reason instead of rewriting the file.

findings.title: `review-queue.json` updates are still unlocked full-file rewrites, so the new sweep introduces fresh lost-update races with `server.mjs` and the nightly cron
findings.recommendation: Add a lock/CAS protocol around queue mutations before adding another writer. At minimum, `runReapplySweep`, `doRunNow()`, and the cron path need serialized access or baseline-checked writes so apply/reject/reapply transitions cannot clobber each other.

findings.title: The new JSON log helper silently drops prior history on corruption and loses entries under concurrent appends
findings.recommendation: Stop converting parse/read failures into an empty log before append. Preserve the existing file on error, surface a distinct failure, and use append-safe/atomic writes (or locking) so concurrent cron/UI runs cannot truncate each other's log entries.
